relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: At deep dawn, the women come with prepared spices.
The stone is already rolled away.
The body is not in the tomb.
Perplexity meets dazzling messengers.
They ask why the living one is sought among the dead.
He is raised; memory must return to Galilee’s words.
The Son of Humanity’s handing-over, cross, and third-day rising are remembered.
His words return to them.
The women return as witnesses to the eleven and the rest.
Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary, and the other women speak to the apostles.
Their witness is dismissed as nonsense.
Peter runs, sees linen, and marvels.
Two travel toward Emmaus from Jerusalem.
They speak together about what happened.
Jesus draws near and walks with them.
Their eyes are held from recognition.
He asks about their words, and sadness stops them.
Cleopas wonders how this stranger does not know.
They name Jesus as prophet powerful in work and word.
They tell of handing-over and crucifixion.
Their hope for Israel’s redemption is bruised on the third day.
The women’s tomb witness astonished them.
The women reported messengers saying he lives.
Others confirmed the empty tomb but did not see him.
Jesus names slow hearts to trust the prophets.
Messiah’s suffering and glory are necessary.
Moses and all the prophets are opened around him.
He appears to go farther at the village.
They urge him to remain as evening falls.
At table, bread is blessed, broken, and given.
Eyes open in the breaking of bread.
Their hearts burned as Scripture opened on the way.
They return that same hour to Jerusalem.
The gathered ones confess the Lord is truly raised.
They tell how he was known in broken bread.
Jesus stands among them with peace.
Fear mistakes presence for apparition.
He questions troubled reasoning in the heart.
Hands, feet, touch, flesh, and bones witness embodied resurrection.
A textual tradition emphasizes showing hands and feet.
Joy still struggles to trust, and he asks for food.
They give him broiled fish.
He eats before them.
Moses, Prophets, and Psalms find fulfillment in him.
Their minds are opened to understand Scripture.
Messiah suffers and rises on the third day.
Turning and release are proclaimed to all nations from Jerusalem.
They are witnesses.
The Father’s promise will clothe them with power from on high.
He leads them to Bethany and blesses them.
Blessing becomes departure into heaven.
They return to Jerusalem with great joy.
The Gospel ends in the temple blessing the Divine.
